evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: monitors data quality and governance including GDPR compliance

evidence.domain_risk: monitors data quality and governance including GDPR compliance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: GDPR compliance is named but the JD is otherwise generic retail analytics without financial-reporting or regulatory-audit specifics.
evidence.data_team_maturity: designs datamodels in dbt and BigQuery
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools already in use (dbt, BigQuery, Looker) with a named Lead Data contact suggest a growing, mid-maturity function.
evidence.jd_authorship: designs datamodels in dbt and BigQuery; resolving pipeline issues
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools (dbt, BigQuery, Looker) and named contact give hiring-manager-level detail.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: works with stakeholders on information needs
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role sits within the internal data function, translating business questions into dashboards for internal consumers.
evidence.autonomy_level: promoting data-driven culture across the organization
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Culture-promotion language is strategic but paired with execution-level dashboard-building and pipeline-fixing work.
evidence.ai_role: interest in advanced analytics and AI applications
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Only a vague 'interest in AI applications' phrase appears, not a concrete AI tool-use or AI-infrastructure expectation.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality monitoring is mentioned as a duty but without a dedicated ownership/framework verb, closer to a listed practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: monitors data quality and governance including GDPR compliance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: GDPR compliance appears once as a monitoring duty, secondary to the dashboard/delivery framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Language of maintaining datamodels and resolving pipeline issues points to fix/scale of existing infrastructure.
evidence.loss_aversion: GDPR compliance appears as a named monitoring responsibility, but is secondary to dashboard and pipeline delivery.
